steps.title: Isosceles triangles have two equal angles
steps.content: An isosceles triangle is defined by having at least two equal angles. Since we're given that angle R = 29°, triangle RST is isosceles if either angle S = 29° (matching R) or if angle S = angle T (making the two non-R angles equal).

steps.title: Test whether S = T is possible
steps.content: If angles S and T are equal, all three angles must sum to 180°. Setting up the equation: $$29° + S + S = 180°$$ which simplifies to $$2S = 151°,$$ or $$S = 75.5°.$$ Since none of the answer choices gives $S = 75.5°$, the two non-R angles cannot be equal.

steps.title: S must equal R for the triangle to be isosceles
steps.content: Since S ≠ T, we need S = R = 29° for the triangle to be isosceles. Looking at the choices, only choice D states angle S = 29°. Let's verify: if R = 29° and S = 29°, then T = 180° − 29° − 29° = 122°. All three angles sum to 180° ✓, and R = S ✓, confirming the triangle is isosceles. That matches choice D — D is correct.
